dimensions.name: Requirements Met
dimensions.gaps_to_10: All 6 stated requirements are satisfied: single useEffect with cleanup flag eliminates StrictMode double-invocation, audioManager.preloadWords called on mount, verify-assets.ts prints PASS/FAIL per word, exits 1 on missing image naming the file, prebuild script added, all 300 assets verified passing.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.gaps_to_10: DB-fetch branch has try/catch with console.warn and isLoading fallback. The wordList prop branch lacks a try/catch (none needed since it is synchronous state assignment), but the async getSetting call is guarded with .catch(()=>{}). The only gap: when db===null and wordList is also absent/empty, the component exits load() early and sets isLoading(false), rendering 'No words found' — correct behavior but could be clearer (e.g., log a dev warning).

dimensions.name: UX Completeness
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Loading spinner shown while data is fetching, 'No words found' empty state shown correctly, audio preloads on mount so first tap has no multi-second delay. Minor gap: no visual indicator if audio preloading fails (but audioManager logs a console.warn on load error, which is acceptable).

dimensions.name: Code Quality
dimensions.gaps_to_10: The merged useEffect is clean, correctly scoped, and the dependency array [db, wordList] is accurate. Minor issue: the verify-assets.ts table uses padEnd(8) for the Image column and padEnd(12) for audio columns, but a FAIL string like 'FAIL (/images/vocabulary/butterfly.png)' is 36+ chars and overflows the column, destroying table alignment when assets are missing. The script still functions correctly (exits 1, names the file), but the table is cosmetically broken in the failure case.

dimensions.name: Test Coverage
dimensions.gaps_to_10: All 7 existing ExploreCards tests pass. The Howler mock is in place so audioManager.preloadWords does not throw. Gap: no explicit assertion that preloadWords was called with the correct words after the component mounts with the wordList prop (e.g., jest.spyOn(audioManager, 'preloadWords')). The DB-loading branch of the merged effect is also untested (useDB is mocked to return null in all tests). These are acceptable gaps for a refactor per the plan, but explicit preloadWords call verification would give higher confidence.

dimensions.name: Security
dimensions.gaps_to_10: No hardcoded API keys. No user input flows into the new code. verify-assets.ts only reads from VOCABULARY_SEED (static seed data) and the filesystem. No auth surface changes.

issues.description: Table column widths are too narrow for FAIL status strings. The Image column uses padEnd(8) and audio columns use padEnd(12), but a FAIL string such as 'FAIL (/images/vocabulary/butterfly.png)' is 38 characters. When assets are missing the table columns run together, making it harder to scan. In the current passing state this is invisible, but the next developer who triggers a failure will see a broken table.
issues.fix_guidance: Either increase the padEnd values (e.g., 40 for each status column) or print only the filename on separate lines when status is FAIL. Example: const imageStatus = imageOk ? 'PASS' : 'FAIL'; and then on the next line print `  missing: ${word.imagePath}` indented.

issues.description: No explicit assertion that audioManager.preloadWords is called when ExploreCards mounts with a wordList prop. The call does not throw (Howler is mocked), but there is no expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalledWith(testWords) guard. If a future refactor accidentally removes the preloadWords call, no test would catch it, allowing the audio-preloading regression to reach production silently.
issues.fix_guidance: Add a jest.spyOn(audioManager, 'preloadWords') before the render call and assert expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalledWith(testWords) after render. This matches the pattern already used in ListenAndFind.test.tsx where jest.spyOn(audioManager, 'playWordEn') is used to assert audio behavior.

summary: The implementation is production-ready. Both duplicate useEffects are correctly merged into a single effect with a cancellation flag that prevents setState on unmounted components and eliminates React StrictMode double-invocation warnings. audioManager.preloadWords is wired in both the prop-supplied and DB-fetched code paths, satisfying the immediate-audio requirement. The verify-assets.ts script is correct, covers all 300 assets (100 words × 3 files), and all pass. The prebuild hook is correctly wired. All 7 ExploreCards tests and the full 212-test suite pass. Two P2 issues exist: (1) table column padding in verify-assets.ts overflows FAIL strings, making the error table hard to read when assets are missing; (2) no explicit spy assertion for the preloadWords call in ExploreCards tests. Neither issue affects runtime user experience or production correctness.
